reasoning: SPY at 707.99 after a +3.85 gap-up open, now resting 81¢ below the premarket high of 708.80. The battle level is PMH 708.80 — the immediate resistance ceiling. Ribbon is BULL (fast 708.24 / pivot 708.03 / slow 707.46, 78¢ spread), providing support for a break attempt. If PMH holds on first test, range-bound intraday (704.08 PML to 708.80 PMH). If PMH breaks with volume, next resistance at 711.28 (prior RTH high) and 712.39 (5-day RTH high). Downside floor is solid at 704.08 (PML); below that exposes 702.64 (prior RTH low) and ultimately 694.2 (5-day Carry support). Asymmetry: more room to run up (4.40 to 5-day high) than down (only 3.91 to PML before gap-fill acceleration). Bias is bullish but conditional on PMH hold/break behavior at market open.

primary.condition: if SPY holds 708.80 PMH on first test during RTH open
primary.entry_trigger: level_reclaim at PMH + ribbon stack BULL confirmation + volume ≥ 1.5x

secondary.condition: if SPY breaks above 708.80 with conviction (body > 10¢ above PMH)
secondary.entry_trigger: breakout above PMH + ribbon alignment holding

tertiary.condition: if SPY reverses and breaks below 704.08 PML
tertiary.entry_trigger: level_break below PML + ribbon inversion OR volume spike on rejection

key_observations: Price is 81¢ below PMH, ideal distance for first test without being forced; natural confluence point for early RTH decision
Ribbon BULL stack with 78¢ spread provides strong support — price is above all three moving averages, momentum intact
Asymmetric risk/reward: 4.40 to 5-day high (712.39) vs 3.91 to PML (704.08) — bulls have more room to run
5-day Carry support (694.2) and 5-day Carry resistance (712.39) frame the daily range; today is a test of the 5-day high structure
Round numbers (707, 706, 705) between current price and PMH provide awareness-only micro-resistance but not hard stops
VIX falling at 19.11 in MID regime — not a high-volatility environment, favors directional continuation over reversals
